# LeShell

> Translate natural language to shell commands and execute them in isolated Docker sandbox containers. Powered by a fine-tuned 800M parameter model.

Built by LeSearch AI (https://lesearch.ai)

## API Endpoints

### Translate (Natural Language to Bash)
- Method: POST /api/translate
- Body: {"query": "find all python files modified today"}
- Response: {"command": "find . -name '*.py' -mtime 0", "meta": "model: nl2shell-0.8b | latency: 0.3s"}
- Rate Limit: 30 requests/min per IP

### Execute (Run in Sandbox)
- Method: POST /api/execute
- Body: {"sessionId": "uuid", "command": "echo hello", "timeout_ms": 30000}
- Response: {"stdout": "hello\n", "stderr": "", "exitCode": 0, "durationMs": 5, "auditId": "..."}
- Rate Limit: 10 requests/min per IP
- Requires: Active sandbox session

### Session Management
- Create: POST /api/session → {"id": "uuid", "status": "running", "createdAt": 1234567890}
- List: GET /api/session → [...]
- Get: GET /api/session/{id}
- Delete: DELETE /api/session/{id}

### MCP (Model Context Protocol)
- Endpoint: POST /api/mcp
- Protocol: MCP Streamable HTTP (JSON-RPC 2.0)
- Tools: nl2shell_translate, sandbox_execute, sandbox_session_create, sandbox_read_file, sandbox_write_file

## Security

- Sandbox containers run with: --network none, --cap-drop ALL, --memory 512m
- 13 dangerous command patterns are blocked (rm -rf, fork bombs, docker escape, etc.)
- All tool calls are logged to a JSONL audit trail
- Sessions auto-expire after 24 hours of inactivity

## Integration

Add to your MCP config:
```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "leshell": {
      "url": "https://your-domain.com/api/mcp"
    }
  }
}
```

## Source
- GitHub: https://github.com/aryateja2106/nl2shell-web
- Model: https://huggingface.co/AryaYT/nl2shell-0.8b
